text: This animation demonstrates overfitting in neural networks.
As training progresses, the training loss, shown in blue, continuously decreases.
However, the validation loss, shown in red, decreases initially but then starts to rise.
This divergence point marks where overfitting begins. The model starts memorizing the training data rather than learning generalizable patterns.
The growing gap between training and validation loss indicates poor generalization to new, unseen data.
This is why we need regularization and early stopping to prevent overfitting.
